The phrase "always" directly translates to the LTL operator G (Globally). The statement "prop_0 is always prop_1" means that at all times, if prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must also hold. This 'if-then' relationship is represented by the implication operator '->'.

The phrase "A always B" translates to the LTL formula G(A -> B), meaning that it is always the case that if A holds, then B also holds. In this specific case, "prop_0 always prop_1" means that it is always true that if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must also be true.
